# pg-kinetic

> pg-kinetic is a PostgreSQL wire proxy for connection pooling, conservative session handling, read routing, admin inspection, health checks, metrics, and regression tooling.

Use this file as the compact reference for agents and LLM crawlers. Canonical documentation is hosted at https://docs.pgkinetic.dev/.

## Current Runtime Status

- Implemented runtime behavior: PostgreSQL wire proxying, conservative backend reuse, connection pooling, read-routing primitives, admin `SHOW` views, HTTP health endpoints, Prometheus-style metrics, TLS/authentication controls, Docker Compose assets, and a local Helm chart.
- Preview or offline tooling: sharding models, policy models, mirroring models, compatibility reports, benchmarks, profiling, and regression workflows.
- Not implemented as live traffic features today: HTTP `/drain`, Kubernetes operator or CRDs, live sharding traffic configuration, live policy enforcement, and live mirroring traffic.
- Current installation path before the first release tag: build `pg-kinetic:local` from the repository checkout and use the local Docker Compose or Helm chart assets.

- Quickstart: https://docs.pgkinetic.dev/quickstart

- Benchmarking: https://docs.pgkinetic.dev/benchmarking
- Regression workflow: https://docs.pgkinetic.dev/regression
- GitHub repository: https://github.com/hookwoods/pg-kinetic

## Entity Terms

- Product name: pg-kinetic
- Category: PostgreSQL wire proxy, PostgreSQL connection pooler, database proxy
- Runtime language: Rust
- Primary audience: PostgreSQL operators, platform engineers, backend engineers, and database reliability teams
- Public domain: docs.pgkinetic.dev
